bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me only moderate utility, and with no messages or concession pattern yet, I will anchor on my strongest values across the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s second offer is actually worse for my utility than their first, so I do not read meaningful concession from them; I will only concede on low-weight issueD, where they have consistently proposed valueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.93 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers have not improved for me; in fact, their utility to me has declined from about 0.44 to 0.35. Since they have repeatedly offered issueD: valueC and issueB: valueB, I am only making a small concession on the low-weight issueD while holding firm on the most valuable issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent’s valid offers remain low for me, around 0.35–0.44 utility, without a clear upward concession trend. The opponent consistently offers issueD: valueC, which is my lowest-weight issue, so I concede there while holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.08 it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers have not shown a clear concession trend in my favor, staying around low-to-mid utility for me. I am targeting a high-utility bid while conceding only on the lowest-weight issueD to avoid repeating my previous exact offer and to test flexibility there.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for myself while making a small concession on the lowest-weight issueD. The opponent’s offers remain low-value to me and fluctuate rather than clearly conceding, though they repeatedly choose issueD: valueC, so I can concede there without giving up much utility.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me while making a small concession only on the low-weight issueD. The opponent’s latest offer conceded issueB to my top value, but they continue to resist my preferred issueA and issueC values; I will concede only the low-weight issueD to valueC, which they frequently offer.

At t=0.24 it is still early enough to target a high-utility bid for me, while making only a small concession on the lowest-weight issue.
